items.curatedReason: 对于脊髓损伤（SCI）患者，食物不安全（FI）是一个不容忽视的问题。这项观察研究提示，在常规康复计划之外，你还需要筛查患者的社会经济和营养状况，因为 FI 不仅普遍（比对照组高出近3倍），还与更高的BMI相关，可能影响整体健康和康复预后。
items.titleZh: 脊髓损伤个体食物不安全的社会人口学决定因素及其与 BMI 的关联：来自“我们所有人”研究项目的横断面分析
items.summaryZh: “我们所有人”研究项目的一项横断面分析比较了脊髓损伤（SCI）成年人与匹配对照组中食物不安全（FI）的患病率，确定了 FI 的相关因素，并探讨了其与 BMI 的关联。
items.curatedReasonEn: Food insecurity (FI) is a significant concern for individuals with spinal cord injury (SCI). This observational study suggests that beyond standard rehabilitation, you should screen patients for socioeconomic and nutritional status. FI is prevalent (nearly 3 times higher than controls) and associated with higher BMI in this population, potentially impacting overall health and rehabilitation outcomes.
items.limitation: 本研究为横断面分析，无法建立食物不安全与 BMI 之间的因果关系。
items.limitationEn: This cross-sectional analysis cannot establish a causal relationship between food insecurity and BMI.
items.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation

items.title: The psychosocial impacts of having eating and drinking difficulties after stroke: a qualitative evidence synthesis.
items.summary: A thematic synthesis of 22 studies (n=645 informants) revealed profound psychological and social impacts of eating and drinking difficulties after stroke, extending beyond physical impairment to affect emotional well-being and social participation.
items.curatedReason: 这项系统综述深入揭示了卒中后吞咽困难（进食饮水障碍）对患者和照护者心理社会影响的广度和深度。这提醒您在康复评估和干预中，不能仅关注生理功能，更要整合对患者情绪、社会参与和照护者负担的全面支持，以提升整体生活质量。
items.titleZh: 卒中后进食饮水困难的心理社会影响：一项定性证据综合
items.summaryZh: 一项对 22 项研究（n=645 受访者）的专题综合分析揭示，卒中后进食饮水困难的心理社会影响深远，超越了身体损伤，影响到情绪健康和社会参与。
items.curatedReasonEn: This systematic review deeply reveals the breadth and depth of psychosocial impacts of post-stroke dysphagia (eating and drinking difficulties) on patients and caregivers. This reminds you that rehabilitation assessment and intervention must not only focus on physiological function but also integrate comprehensive support for patients' emotions, social participation, and caregiver burden to improve overall quality of life.
items.limitation: 本综述纳入的研究主要为定性研究，结果可能存在解释偏倚，且不同研究的背景和方法可能存在异质性。
items.limitationEn: The studies included in this review are primarily qualitative, which may introduce interpretation bias, and there might be heterogeneity in the background and methods of different studies.

items.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
items.summary: An exploratory retrospective cohort study (n=333) found that sarcopenia increased the risk of adjacent segment degeneration within 3 years of anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF).
items.curatedReason: 这项回顾性队列研究提示，肌少症会增加前路颈椎间盘切除融合术（ACDF）后三年内发生邻近节段退变的风险。这意味着您在术前评估老年患者时，应筛查肌少症，并可能需要将针对肌少症的干预纳入围手术期管理，以降低术后并发症风险。
items.titleZh: 肌少症增加前路颈椎间盘切除融合术后 3 年内邻近节段退变风险
items.summaryZh: 一项探索性回顾性队列研究（n=333）发现，肌少症增加了前路颈椎间盘切除融合术（ACDF）后 3 年内邻近节段退变的风险。
items.curatedReasonEn: This retrospective cohort study suggests that sarcopenia increases the risk of adjacent segment degeneration within three years following anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF). This means you should screen for sarcopenia when assessing elderly patients preoperatively and potentially incorporate sarcopenia-targeted interventions into perioperative management to reduce postoperative complication risks.
items.limitation: 本研究是回顾性队列研究，可能存在选择偏倚和混杂因素，不能完全排除其他未测量变量对结果的影响，且肌少症的诊断标准可能影响结果。
items.limitationEn: This retrospective cohort study may have selection bias and confounding factors, and cannot fully exclude the influence of other unmeasured variables. The diagnostic criteria for sarcopenia might also affect the results.

items.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
items.summary: A systematic review with meta-analysis (17 trials, n=1,372 participants) found that shortwave diathermy significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [-1.02, -0.42]; p < .01) in patients with knee osteoarthritis.
items.curatedReason: 短波透热（SWD）对膝骨关节炎患者的疼痛和行走能力有显著改善，这是基于 17 项 RCT、1372 名患者的有力证据。你可以放心地将 SWD 纳入你的膝关节骨性关节炎治疗方案，尤其在疼痛管理方面，它能提供可靠的即时效果。
items.titleZh: 短波透热疗法治疗膝骨关节炎：一项系统综述和荟萃分析
items.summaryZh: 一项系统综述和荟萃分析（17项试验，1372名参与者）发现，短波透热疗法显著改善了膝骨关节炎患者的疼痛（平均值 -2.13；95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]；p < .01）和行走能力（平均值 -0.72；95% CI [-1.02, -0.42]；p < .01）。
items.curatedReasonEn: Shortwave diathermy (SWD) significantly improves pain and walking ability in knee osteoarthritis patients, backed by strong evidence from 17 RCTs and 1,372 participants. You can confidently integrate SWD into your KOA treatment protocols, especially for pain management, where it offers reliable immediate effects.
items.limitation: 尽管有显著改善，但未报告异质性（I² statistic）的具体数值，可能存在不同研究间的差异性，影响结果的一致性。且部分研究质量可能不高，导致证据等级受限。
items.limitationEn: Although significant improvements were reported, the specific value of heterogeneity (I² statistic) was not provided, suggesting potential variability among studies which might affect consistency. The quality of some included studies might also be low, limiting the overall strength of evidence.
